slides.title: Stack 📚
slides.content:
# Stack

`Stack` allows you to place children on top of each other (like a stack of papers).

* Children are painted in order: first child at the bottom, last at the top.
* Use `Positioned` to place a child at a specific offset from the stack's edges.
* If not positioned, children are aligned according to `alignment`.

quizData.question: Which widget would you use to overlay text on an image?
quizData.explanation: Stack lets you place widgets on top of each other, perfect for overlays.

slides.title: Stack with Positioned
slides.content: An image with a caption at the bottom:

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## Use `Positioned.fill`

To make a child fill the entire stack, use `Positioned.fill(child: ...)`.
